zh.lifeSummary: Freiherr Hermann III von Hirscher是Georg Baron Von Hirscher II和Frau Georg II von Hirscher的兒子，於1102年出生在德國巴登-爾特波格藍德可萊斯 拉文斯包格波德格希爾黑。他和Katerina of Hirscher-Sargans至少有1個兒子和2個女兒。他去世於1157年在他的家鄉享年55歲。

nameMeanings.name: von
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Cambodian: written វ៉ុន, unexplained.

variants.name: Vaughan
meanings: Welsh: from fychan, a lenited form of bychan, a diminutive of bach ‘little’. This was often used to distinguish the younger of two bearers of the same personal name, typically the son of a father with the same name.

nameMeanings.name: Hirscher
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Gunter, Hartmut, Herta, Kurt, Otto, Wolf.
German: occupational name for a keeper of deer, from Middle High German hir(t)z ‘deer’ + the agent suffix -ler.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): patronymic from a pet form of   Hirsch   2.

variants.name: Hirsch
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Emanuel, Meyer, Shimon, Moshe, Yosef, Beril, Chaim, Elihu, Eliyohu.  German Kurt, Otto, Erwin, Hans, Heinz, Helmut, Beate, Fritz, Gunther, Hedwig, Hilde, Ilse.

German: from Middle High German hir(t)z ‘deer, stag’; a metonymic occupational name for a keeper of deer, a nickname for someone thought to resemble a deer or stag, or a topographic or habitational name referring to a house distinguished by the sign of a stag. It is also found in some central European countries, for example in Czechia and Slovenia, often as a translation into German of corresponding Slavic surnames, e.g. Jelen . In some cases this is also a standardized form of the Gottscheerish (i.e. Gottschee German) surname Hiris .

Jewish (Ashkenazic): from the Yiddish male personal name Hirsh ‘deer’, which is common because of the association of the deer with the Hebrew personal name Naphtali, deriving from the blessing by Jacob of his sons (Genesis 49: 21), in which Naphtali is referred to as a ‘hind let loose’. Compare Deer , Jelen , Jelinek , and Jelonek .

nameMeanings.name: Hermann
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

nameMeanings.name: III
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Japanese Yuko, Aiko, Hideaki, Hiroki, Hiroyuki, Isamu, Kaoru, Katsumi, Keiichiro, Kenji, Masako, Masayuki.
Japanese: variously written, sometimes with characters used phonetically; the most probable meanings are 仁井 ‘benevolent well’, 新居 ‘new residence’, or 新井 ‘new well’. It is not common in Japan.

en.lifeSummary: Freiherr Hermann III von Hirscher was born in 1102, in Hirscher, Bodnegg, Ravensburg, Baden-Württemberg, Germany as the son of Georg Baron Von Hirscher II and Frau Georg II von Hirscher. He had at least 1 son and 2 daughters with Katerina of Hirscher-Sargans. He died in 1157, in his hometown, at the age of 55.

ko.lifeSummary: Freiherr Hermann III von Hirscher(은)는 1102년 독일 바데-wu르테버그 랜드크레이스 레이븐스버그 보드네그 허슈어에서 Georg Baron Von Hirscher II(와)과 Frau Georg II von Hirscher의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 Katerina of Hirscher-Sargans(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1157년 그의 고향에서 55세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Freiherr Hermann III von Hirscher родился в 1102 г. в местности хирщир, боднег, ландкрис равенсбург, баден-вuрттембирг, Германия. Его родителей звали Georg Baron Von Hirscher II и Frau Georg II von Hirscher. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери от Katerina of Hirscher-Sargans. Он умер в 1157 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 55 лет.
